stem: A worker's annual salary was $60,000 last year. This year, the worker received a 5% raise. If the worker saves 20% of the new salary, how much does the worker save this year?

distractorRationale.A: Student applied 20% savings to the old salary instead of the new one: $60,000 × 0.20 = $12,000.
distractorRationale.C: Student added the percentages (5% + 20% = 25%) and applied to the original: $60,000 × 0.25 = $15,000.
distractorRationale.D: Student calculated only the raise amount: $60,000 × 0.05 = $3,000.
